subject: Geography
title: Mountain Peoples
description: A short lesson to help you apply Mountain Peoples.

hook.text: Sherpas carry heavy loads at extreme altitude because their blood carries oxygen more efficiently.

buildup.text: High-altitude peoples in Tibet, Ethiopia, and the Andes each evolved different adaptations.

discovery.text: Tibetans have a gene from ancient Denisovans that helps them thrive with less oxygen.

twist.text: Andean peoples developed larger lungs and hearts, while Ethiopians process oxygen faster.

climax.text: Three mountain populations found three different solutions to the same problem.

punchline.text: Same challenge, three different evolutionary answers.

quiz.question: Where did Tibetans get their high-altitude gene?
